questions.prompt_html:
An object moves along the x-axis so that at time t seconds (where t ≥ 0) it is x =  m to the right of the origin. By calculating the average velocity of the object over time intervals [4, 4 + h] for h = 0.1, h = 0.01, 
h = 0.001, and h = 0.0001, determine the velocity of the particle at time t = 4s.
questions.prompt_text: An object moves along the x-axis so that at time t seconds (where t ≥ 0) it is x = square root of (t) m to the right of the origin. By calculating the average velocity of the object over time intervals [4, 4 + h] for h = 0.1, h = 0.01, h = 0.001, and h = 0.0001, determine the velocity of the particle at time t = 4s.

questions.prompt_text: In the following figure the curve is the graph of a quantity y that is a function of time t. Also shown is its tangent line at t = 100. What is the average rate of change of y from t = 20 to t = 160? What is the approximate rate of change of y with respect to t at t = 100? Short Description: A graph of a function shows a concave up curve with a tangent line to the curve. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 40 to 200 in increments of 20, and the y axis ranges from negative 30 to 90 in increments of 10. A concave up smooth curve passes through the points (negative 40, 60), (0, 28), (20, 18), (100, 18), and (160, 60). A tangent line to curve passes through the point (100, 18) on the curve and intersects the x axis approximately at (55, 0).

classification.ap_topic_title: Exploring Types of Discontinuities
classification.crosswalk_note: Identifies jump discontinuities and one-sided continuity in a step function.
classification.framework: AP Calculus Course and Exam Description, current Unit 1 topic framework

questions.prompt_html:
Courier rates for delivery of mail up to 200 g are given in the following chart:



Draw the graph of the cost (in dollars) of mailing a letter as a function of its mass (in grams). Where are the discontinuities of this function? Is the cost function right or left continuous at these points?
questions.prompt_text: Courier rates for delivery of mail up to 200 g are given in the following chart: matrix ((Up to and including 30 g 50 g 100 g 200 g)(Mailing cost $0.38 $0.59 $0.76 $1.14)) Draw the graph of the cost (in dollars) of mailing a letter as a function of its mass (in grams). Where are the discontinuities of this function? Is the cost function right or left continuous at these points?

choices.label: A
choices.html: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; left but not right continuous there
choices.text: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; left but not right continuous there

choices.label: B
choices.html: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; neither left nor right continuous there
choices.text: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; neither left nor right continuous there

choices.label: C
choices.html: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; right continuous at 30 and 50, left continuous at 100
choices.text: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; right continuous at 30 and 50, left continuous at 100

choices.label: D
choices.html: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; right but not left continuous there
choices.text: discontinuous at 30, 50, 100; right but not left continuous there

images.alt: matrix ((Up to and including 	30 g 	50 g 	100 g 	200 g)(Mailing cost 	$0.38 	$0.59 	$0.76 	$1.14))

questions.prompt_html:
Let g(x) =   

where k is a constant real number. If g has a removable discontinuity at x = 2, then k is equal to:

The second curve is a concave up curve in the second quadrant passing through the point (negative 2.5, 1.5). The upper end of the curve approaches a dashed vertical line at x = negative 2 and the lower end approach a dashed horizontal line at y = 1. The third curve is an inverted U shaped curve with top at (0, 0.5). The left end of the curve approaches the dashed vertical line at x = negative 2 and the right end approaches the dashed vertical line at x = 2.

questions.prompt_html:
A girl is on the bank of a river that is 1 kilometre wide. She wants to travel to a town on the opposite bank 
but 1 kilometre upstream. She intends to row in a straight line to some point P on the opposite bank and walk the remaining distance along the (straight) bank. To what point should she row in order to reach her destination in the least possible time if she can walk 5 kilometres per hour and row 3 kilometres per hour?
Refer to the figure below:

questions.prompt_html: If R be the plane region enclosed by the graphs of y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b, where a > 0 (as shown in the figure below), then the volume V of the solid generated by revolving the plane region R about the line x = -2 is V = 2πdx.
questions.prompt_text: If R be the plane region enclosed by the graphs of y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b, where a > 0 (as shown in the figure below), then the volume V of the solid generated by revolving the plane region R about the line x = -2 is V = 2π integral of ((x+2)[f(x) - g(x)]) from (a) to (b) dx. Short Description: A graph shows two curves.Long Description: The graph of y = f of x is a curve that slopes upward with rises and falls. The graph of y = g of x is a curve below the previous curve. The curve concave up on the left and concave down on the right. The points of these two curves are connected by dashed vertical lines on the left and right. Both the curve are between x = a and x = b. The region between the curves is labeled, R. A dashed vertical line left to the y axis is labeled, axis of rotation x = negative 2.

images.alt: Short Description: A graph shows two curves.Long Description: The graph of y = f of x is a curve that slopes upward with rises and falls. A dashed vertical line left to the y axis is labeled, axis of rotation x = negative 2.

choices.label: A

choices.label: B

choices.label: D

choices.label: E

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The wind speed is tracked as a storm approaches. The maximum speed w of wind gusts, in miles per hour, t hours after midnight on the night of an approaching storm is given in the figure.

a. Find the slope of the secant line that passes through points (1, 17.2) and (3, 26.9). Interpret your answer as an average rate of change over the interval 1 ≤ t ≤ 3.
b. Repeat the procedure outlined in part (a) for the secant line that passes through points (7, 34.4) and 
c. Notice that the curve in the figure is generally increasing until t = 6, and is generally decreasing after  Give a plausible explanation for this behavior.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The wind speed is tracked as a storm approaches. The maximum speed w of wind gusts, in miles per hour, t hours after midnight on the night of an approaching storm is given in the figure. a. Find the slope of the secant line that passes through points (1, 17.2) and (3, 26.9). Interpret your answer as an average rate of change over the interval 1 ≤ t ≤ 3. b. Repeat the procedure outlined in part (a) for the secant line that passes through points (7, 34.4) and (8, 31). c. Notice that the curve in the figure is generally increasing until t = 6, and is generally decreasing after t = 6. Give a plausible explanation for this behavior.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
a. 9.7, the wind is increasing in speed at an average rate of 9.7 mph per hour
b. -3.4, the wind speed is decreasing in speed at an average rate of 3.4 mph per hour 
c. The storm reached its closest point about 6:00 a.m. and is moving away after that.

c. The storm reached its closest point about 6:00 a.m. and is moving away after that.
